questions.answer: Human Development Index is a composite index measuring average achievement in three basic dimensions of human development — a long and healthy life, knowledge and a decent standard of living.
Life expectancy at birth is the number of years a newborn infant would live if prevailing patterns of mortality at the time of its birth were to stay the same throughout its life.
Literacy rate is the percentage of literate persons in a given population, aged 7 years and above.

questions.q: What does GDP per capita indicate?
questions.answer: GDP per capita indicates the standard of living of a country, which is the total GDP of a country divided by its population.

questions.q: Name the three dimensions of Human Development Index.
questions.answer: The three dimensions of Human Development Index are: a long and healthy life, knowledge and a decent standard of living.

questions.q: Discuss the importance of life expectancy at birth in Human Development Index.
questions.answer: Life expectancy at birth is an important component of Human Development Index, as it reflects the health status of a country. A high life expectancy at birth indicates better healthcare facilities and services.

questions.q: Explain the concept of literacy rate and its significance in Human Development Index.
questions.answer: Literacy rate is the percentage of literate persons in a given population, aged 7 years and above. It is significant in Human Development Index as it reflects the knowledge dimension of a country.

questions.q: Describe the role of GDP per capita in measuring the standard of living.
questions.answer: GDP per capita is a measure of the total GDP of a country divided by its population. It indicates the standard of living of a country, with higher GDP per capita indicating a higher standard of living.

questions.q: Compare and contrast the Human Development Index of developed and developing countries.
questions.answer: Developed countries have a higher Human Development Index compared to developing countries, indicating better health, education, and standard of living. Developing countries have a lower HDI due to poor healthcare, low literacy rates, and low GDP per capita.

questions.q: Explain the significance of Human Development Index in assessing the development of a country.
questions.answer: Human Development Index is significant in assessing the development of a country as it provides a comprehensive picture of a country's development, beyond economic indicators. It reflects the wellbeing of people and the quality of life.

questions.q: Discuss the factors that affect the Human Development Index of a country, with examples.
questions.answer: The factors that affect the Human Development Index of a country include life expectancy at birth, literacy rate, and GDP per capita. For example, a country with high life expectancy, high literacy rate, and high GDP per capita will have a higher HDI. On the other hand, a country with low life expectancy, low literacy rate, and low GDP per capita will have a lower HDI.

questions.q: Explain the relationship between Human Development Index and economic development, with suitable examples.
questions.answer: There is a positive relationship between Human Development Index and economic development. A country with high economic development tends to have a higher Human Development Index, as it can invest more in healthcare, education, and social services. For example, Norway has a high GDP per capita and also a high HDI, while a country like Burundi has a low GDP per capita and a low HDI.
key_points: Relationship between HDI and economic development
questions.topic: Relationship between HDI and Economic Development

questions.q: Analyze the strengths and weaknesses of Human Development Index as a measure of development.
questions.answer: The strengths of Human Development Index include its comprehensive nature, simplicity, and ease of understanding. However, its weaknesses include its failure to account for factors like income inequality, poverty, and environmental sustainability. Additionally, it can be influenced by factors like data quality and methodology.

questions.q: Discuss the role of education in Human Development Index, with reference to India.
questions.answer: Education plays a crucial role in Human Development Index, as it reflects the knowledge dimension of a country. In India, education has been a major focus area, with the government implementing various initiatives to improve literacy rates and access to education. Despite progress, India still lags behind in terms of education, with low literacy rates and poor quality of education in some areas.

questions.q: Examine the trends in Human Development Index over time, with reference to a developed and a developing country.
questions.answer: The trends in Human Development Index over time show an improvement in most countries, with developed countries maintaining a high HDI and developing countries showing an increase in HDI. For example, Norway has consistently had a high HDI, while a country like India has shown an increase in HDI over time, driven by improvements in life expectancy, literacy rate, and GDP per capita.
